reasoning: Since December 2025, the Effective Federal Funds Rate (EFFR) has been perfectly flat at 3.64%. The next FOMC meeting is on March 17-18, 2026. Therefore, for the March 8 horizon, the rate will almost certainly remain stable, barring an unexpected, highly anomalous 1-2 basis point inter-meeting fluctuation (probability estimated at 7%). For the March 31 horizon, the FOMC could theoretically announce a rate hike (a reversal of their recent cutting/pausing trend, estimated at ~5-10% probability), or there could be a micro-fluctuation upward due to March 31 quarter-end funding/repo dynamics (e.g., in Oct 2025, it fluctuated by 3 bps). Cumulatively, the probability of an increase by March 31 is around 15%, reflecting greater uncertainty over the longer horizon and the inclusion of an FOMC meeting and quarter-end.
